mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession expectations that compress equity multiples and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in consumer price inflation and retail sales indicate demand-pull inflationary pressure that forces aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression, or forward returns remain positive when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity utilization bottlenecks and supply chain constraints that erode corporate margins and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive or neutral when INDPRO rises during late-cycle expansions, or the signal reverses direction in early-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over short horizons.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show negative or flat performance following payroll increases, with Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts cross below 1.2 million units, construction sector contraction signals weakening consumer confidence and reduced credit demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in over 70 percent of months where HOUST drops below 1.2 million units.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in the federal funds rate and consumer price inflation create a stagflationary monetary environment that compresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint effect of rising rates and inflation fails to predict negative returns, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales during a monetary tightening cycle demonstrate consumer resilience against higher borrowing costs, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Retail sales increases during tightening periods coincide with negative equity returns, or the predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.
